gold.rationale: The article is built on a real underlying claim—Pitino advanced to his 14th Sweet 16, his first since 2014—but it misframes the clinching game and historical context with specific event details that are not supported by the source material. The visuals further intensify the misleading framing by using an edited sideline photo and a fully synthetic bracket graphic as if they documented the same current tournament event.

atomic_points.post_summary: St. John's defeated defending national champion UConn 72-68 on Sunday night at Barclays Center to advance Rick Pitino's team to the Sweet 16 for the 14th time in the 2026 NCAA tournament.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Rick Pitino is headed to the Sweet 16 for the 14th time, and St. John's is going with him after a tense 72-68 win over defending national champion UConn on Sunday night at Barclays Center.
atoms: Rick Pitino is advancing to the Sweet 16.
This is Rick Pitino's 14th trip to the Sweet 16.
St. John's won the game.
The final score was 72-68.
UConn was the defending national champion.
The game took place on Sunday night.
The game took place at Barclays Center.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The March result immediately altered the top half of the 2026 NCAA men's tournament bracket, giving the Red Storm a defining tournament moment, sending them to the second weekend and adding another chapter to one of college basketball's deepest March resumes.
atoms: The result occurred in March.
The result altered the top half of the 2026 NCAA men's tournament bracket.
St. John's (the Red Storm) reached the second weekend of the tournament.
This result is described as a defining tournament moment for St. John's.

sentence_level.sentence_text: From the opening tip, Barclays Center felt less like a routine tournament site and more like a full-fledged New York basketball occasion.
atoms: The game started with the opening tip.
Barclays Center felt like a full-fledged New York basketball occasion.
Barclays Center did not feel like a routine tournament site.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Red and white filled the lower bowl, each St. John's run brought another wave of noise, and every late defensive stop seemed to push the arena closer to a roar.
atoms: Red and white colors filled the lower bowl.
St. John's runs generated noise from the crowd.
Late defensive stops increased the crowd's noise level.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Pitino patrolled the sideline in his familiar black suit, calling out matchups and motioning his team into half-court sets as the crowd stood through possession after possession in the closing minutes.
atoms: Rick Pitino wore a black suit.
Pitino called out matchups from the sideline.
Pitino motioned his team into half-court sets.
The crowd stood during possessions in the closing minutes.

sentence_level.sentence_text: When the horn sounded, St. John's players flung their arms up and rushed toward one another near midcourt while fans behind the basket screamed and waved school flags over the railing.
atoms: The horn sounded to end the game.
St. John's players flung their arms up.
St. John's players rushed toward each other near midcourt.
Fans behind the basket screamed.
Fans waved school flags over the railing.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For a fan base that has waited years for a breakthrough on the national March stage, the moment landed as more than one win.
atoms: The fan base has waited years for a breakthrough.
The breakthrough occurred on the national March stage.
The moment was significant to the fan base beyond just a win.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It felt like proof that the rebuild had turned into something more substantial, something with real force in the 2026 bracket and something capable of putting St. John's back at the center of the national conversation.
atoms: The win is seen as proof of a successful rebuild.
The team has real force in the 2026 bracket.
The win puts St. John's back at the center of the national conversation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Main cover visual: Rick Pitino reacts from the sideline beneath NCAA tournament signage as St. John's closes out its Sweet 16-clinching win over UConn at Barclays Center on Sunday night.
atoms: The main cover visual shows Rick Pitino reacting from the sideline.
The visual includes NCAA tournament signage.
The visual depicts St. John's clinching a Sweet 16 win over UConn.
The game occurred at Barclays Center on Sunday night.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Graphic: A scoreboard-style bracket explainer shows St. John's 72-68 win over UConn, the Red Storm's route through the East region, and the next Sweet 16 matchup on the 2026 tournament path.
atoms: A graphic shows a scoreboard-style bracket explainer.
The graphic displays the 72-68 win over UConn.
The graphic shows the Red Storm's route through the East region.
The graphic indicates the next Sweet 16 matchup on the 2026 tournament path.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The milestone belongs as much to Pitino as it does to his team.
atoms: The Sweet 16 milestone is attributed to Rick Pitino.
The Sweet 16 milestone is attributed to the St. John's team.

sentence_level.sentence_text: His 14th trip to the Sweet 16 extends a coaching history that has crossed generations of college basketball, from Providence to Kentucky to Louisville and now St. John's.
atoms: Rick Pitino has 14 trips to the Sweet 16.
Pitino's coaching history includes Providence.
Pitino's coaching history includes Kentucky.
Pitino's coaching history includes Louisville.
Pitino's current team is St. John's.

sentence_level.sentence_text: He had not reached this stage since Louisville's 2014 run, and that gap gave extra weight to the sight of him leading another program into the tournament's second weekend.
atoms: Rick Pitino last reached the Sweet 16 with Louisville in 2014.
The gap since 2014 added weight to this achievement.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Pitino's March record has long been defined by how ready his teams look for the particular demands of tournament basketball: quick turnarounds, disciplined defense, urgency on every possession and the ability to stay composed late.
atoms: Pitino's March record is defined by team readiness for tournament demands.
His teams exhibit quick turnarounds.
His teams exhibit disciplined defense.
His teams show urgency on every possession.
His teams stay composed late in games.

sentence_level.sentence_text: He made deep runs at Kentucky, took Louisville to repeated regional semifinals and beyond, and now has St. John's in the Sweet 16 during a season that has increasingly looked like a full-scale revival.
atoms: Pitino made deep runs at Kentucky.
Pitino took Louisville to repeated regional semifinals.
St. John's is in the Sweet 16 this season.
The season is described as a full-scale revival.

sentence_level.sentence_text: His previous appearances at this stage came across different eras and different leagues, a reminder of why his name still carries so much pull once the bracket tightens.
atoms: Pitino's previous Sweet 16 appearances occurred across different eras.
Pitino's previous Sweet 16 appearances occurred across different leagues.
Pitino's name carries significant influence when the bracket tightens.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For St. John's, the breakthrough has its own historical weight.
atoms: St. John's breakthrough has historical weight.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Red Storm had not made the Sweet 16 since 1985, and that drought had become part of the program's identity, a lingering measure of how far the school had drifted from the national relevance it once expected.
atoms: St. John's last made the Sweet 16 in 1985.
The drought since 1985 became part of the program's identity.
The drought measured the school's drift from expected national relevance.

sentence_level.sentence_text: This run changes the conversation.
atoms: This tournament run changes the national conversation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Instead of focusing on what St. John's used to be, the focus now is on what it has become under Pitino and how dangerous it might be over the tournament's final two weekends.
atoms: The focus has shifted from St. John's past to its current state under Pitino.
The focus is on the team's potential danger over the final two weekends of the tournament.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The shift matters beyond one locker room.
atoms: The impact of this shift extends beyond the St. John's locker room.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Big East has spent the last several years trying to reassert its standing in a changing college sports landscape, and St. John's making a headline-grabbing move through the men's bracket gives the conference another burst of visibility.
atoms: The Big East has spent years trying to reassert its standing.
St. John's made a headline-grabbing move in the men's bracket.
This move gives the Big East conference a burst of visibility.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The league has long marketed itself on coaching, toughness and postseason credibility.
atoms: The Big East markets itself on coaching.
The Big East markets itself on toughness.
The Big East markets itself on postseason credibility.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Pitino and the Red Storm delivered all three Sunday night in a win that looked exactly like the sort of March result the conference wants tied to its brand.
atoms: Pitino and St. John's delivered coaching, toughness, and postseason credibility.
The win occurred on Sunday night.
The win matches the type of March result the Big East wants for its brand.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The game itself followed a familiar tournament pattern, with uneasy stretches, bursts of shot-making and a closing sequence that put every possession under a spotlight.
atoms: The game followed a familiar tournament pattern.
The game included uneasy stretches.
The game included bursts of shot-making.
The closing sequence put every possession under a spotlight.

sentence_level.sentence_text: St. John's defended hard on the perimeter, found enough transition scoring to keep control of the momentum and made the cleaner plays late.
atoms: St. John's defended hard on the perimeter.
St. John's used transition scoring to control momentum.
St. John's made cleaner plays late in the game.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Pitino's team never looked rattled by the stakes. It looked prepared for them, which has been one of the defining traits of his best postseason teams over the years.
atoms: Pitino's team did not look rattled by the stakes.
The team looked prepared for the stakes.
Preparedness is a defining trait of Pitino's best postseason teams.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That is why his legacy continues to grow even at this stage of a career already filled with milestones.
atoms: Pitino's legacy continues to grow.
Pitino's career is already filled with milestones.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fourteen Sweet 16 appearances is more than a tally. It reflects adaptability and staying power.
atoms: Rick Pitino has 14 Sweet 16 appearances.
This number reflects adaptability.
This number reflects staying power.

sentence_level.sentence_text: He has reached this point with different rosters, in different conferences and during different phases of the sport's evolution.
atoms: Pitino reached the Sweet 16 with different rosters.
Pitino reached the Sweet 16 in different conferences.
Pitino reached the Sweet 16 during different phases of the sport's evolution.

sentence_level.sentence_text: This latest trip, attached to St. John's and the energy of this 2026 run, may land differently because of the long wait since his last appearance and because of how quickly he has restored belief around a proud program.
atoms: This trip is attached to St. John's.
This trip is attached to the energy of the 2026 run.
The trip may land differently due to the long wait since his last appearance.
The trip may land differently due to how quickly he restored belief in the program.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Sweet 16 now awaits, and St. John's arrives there as one of the tournament's most compelling stories.
atoms: St. John's is advancing to the Sweet 16.
St. John's is described as one of the tournament's most compelling stories.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The fan base is fully engaged, the bracket has broken in intriguing ways and Pitino again has a team playing meaningful games in late March.
atoms: The fan base is fully engaged.
The tournament bracket has broken in intriguing ways.
Pitino has a team playing meaningful games in late March.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For a coach whose reputation was built on navigating this month, and for a program eager to reclaim its place, Sunday night felt like more than a step forward. It felt like an arrival.
atoms: Pitino's reputation was built on navigating March.
St. John's is eager to reclaim its place.
Sunday night felt like more than a step forward.
Sunday night felt like an arrival.

paragraph_level: Rick Pitino has reached the Sweet 16 for the 14th time in his coaching career, extending a legacy spanning Providence, Kentucky, Louisville, and now St. John's.
St. John's victory over defending champion UConn ended a 41-year Sweet 16 drought dating back to 1985, marking a historic breakthrough for the program.
The win at Barclays Center on Sunday night is framed as a full-scale revival for St. John's, restoring national relevance and shifting the focus from past struggles to current potential.
Rick Pitino's coaching style is characterized by disciplined defense, quick turnarounds, and composure under pressure, traits that defined his deep runs at Kentucky and Louisville.
St. John's advancement provides a significant visibility boost for the Big East conference, aligning with the league's branding around coaching, toughness, and postseason credibility.
The 2026 tournament run is portrayed as a compelling narrative of adaptability and staying power, with the team's success attributed to Pitino's ability to restore belief in a proud program.
The post concludes by framing Sunday night's victory not just as a tournament step, but as a definitive arrival for both Pitino and St. John's in the national conversation.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An infographic celebrating St. John's Sweet 16 advancement. It features a tournament bracket, a photo of Rick Pitino, and text announcing the win over Omaha and the upcoming game against Tennessee. Logos for ESPN and TBS are present.

JOHN'S VS 4 TENNESSEE | THURSDAY, MARCH 27 | 7:09 PM ET | tbs | SJ ST. JOHN'S BASKETBALL | NEW YORK TOUGH. PITINO PROVEN. We're Just Getting Started.

retrieved_evidence.text:
🚨BREAKING: Rick Pitino is advancing to his FOURTEENTH Sweet 16...

His first appearance since he took Louisville in 2014

Rick Pitino’s latest advancement to the Sweet 16 ends a notable gap in his postseason résumé, according to tournament records reviewed this week. School and league officials noted that the veteran coach had not reached the regional semifinal stage since his tenure at Louisville, when he last did so in 2014. People familiar with his career trajectory said the long interval underscores the changing landscape of college basketball over the past decade, while also lending additional significance to his current run, which is being closely watched by commentators across the sport.

Rick Pitino’s progress in the 2026 NCAA men’s tournament has renewed attention on a coaching career that has included multiple high-profile stops and repeated success in March, according to analysts. Over several decades, Pitino has led programs such as Providence, Kentucky and Louisville to deep postseason runs, accumulating numerous conference titles and Final Four appearances along the way. College basketball historians say his pattern of guiding different schools into the later rounds of the tournament has established him as one of the sport’s most recognizable sideline figures, with his current role at St. John’s seen as the latest chapter in that record.

Veteran college basketball coach Rick Pitino has guided his team into the regional semifinals of the NCAA men’s tournament for what officials said is the 14th time in his career. The accomplishment, confirmed by tournament statisticians following this weekend’s second-round action, places Pitino among a small group of active coaches with double-digit appearances at this stage. According to people around the program, the achievement is being viewed internally as another benchmark in a coaching career that has spanned several decades and multiple conferences, with further tests expected in the coming Sweet 16 round.

Rick Pitino, one of college basketball’s most storied coaches, has been described in recent reports pacing the sideline in a charged March atmosphere as St. John’s secured what is presented as a 2026 NCAA Tournament victory to reach the Sweet 16. According to accounts from the St. John’s program, the veteran coach, now in his fourteenth regional semifinal overall, is often shown in a dark suit with the school’s red-and-white color scheme surrounding him, reacting animatedly to defensive stops and late-game runs in an arena dressed in familiar March Madness branding and tournament floor markings.

Rick Pitino’s earlier coaching stops, particularly his tenure at Louisville, are often revisited in background sections that trace the arc of his fourteen Sweet 16 appearances. In accounts focusing on his previous deep runs, observers recall him on the Cardinals’ sideline during the mid-2010s, when Louisville regularly advanced into the second weekend of the NCAA Tournament, including a notable regional semifinal trip in 2014. According to historical summaries, those years cemented Pitino’s reputation for tournament preparedness, providing a contrast point to his current chapter at St. John’s and the renewed discussion of his total Sweet 16 tally.

The latest NCAA tournament victory by St. John’s under coach Rick Pitino has injected new momentum into the program’s fan base, according to team officials and spectators interviewed over the weekend. The win, which secured the Red Storm a place in the Sweet 16 of the 2026 men’s bracket, prompted extended celebrations on campus and in local venues around the city. Commentators noted that the performance has reinforced Pitino’s longstanding reputation as a coach whose teams often elevate their play in March, a perception that some observers say may further raise expectations for the remainder of the tournament.

St. John’s has been identified by school officials as the latest program to be associated with Rick Pitino’s milestone trip to a 14th Sweet 16, a development that is being described within the athletic department as a significant step in the program’s broader resurgence. Administrators and longtime observers said in recent days that the second-round victory, which secured the Red Storm’s place in the regional semifinals, is seen as evidence that the men’s basketball team is regaining national relevance. According to early assessments, the result is expected to bolster recruiting efforts and institutional support going into future seasons.

In coverage of St. John’s 2026 postseason under Rick Pitino, commentators frequently reference bracket graphics that trace the Red Storm’s supposed progress through the NCAA Tournament. These stylized diagrams, echoing the official March Madness layout with regional labels and seed lines, typically highlight the second-round matchup, a specific final score and the announced Sweet 16 opponent, emphasizing Pitino’s guidance through successive rounds. According to tournament analysts, such bracket overviews help contextualize the claimed path into the second weekend and underscore that this appearance is being counted as his fourteenth Sweet 16 at the Division I level.

St. John’s University, commonly referred to as the Red Storm, is represented in national coverage by a distinctive athletics logo featuring bold lettering and the program’s red-and-white identity. In recent reports about the school’s 2026 NCAA Tournament run under head coach Rick Pitino, this emblem is used as a concise marker of the team’s renewed presence on the national stage. Observers note that the logo, long associated with the Queens-based institution’s basketball tradition, has become closely tied to the narrative of St. John’s re-emergence in March Madness conversations and Pitino’s accumulation of Sweet 16 appearances.

The 2026 NCAA men’s basketball tournament has moved into its second weekend, with organizers confirming that the full Sweet 16 field is now set following the conclusion of round-of-32 games. According to the NCAA, this year’s bracket has produced a mix of traditional powers and resurgent programs, adding to anticipation for the regional semifinals scheduled across multiple host cities later this week. Officials indicated that television viewership and ticket demand for the Sweet 16 remain strong, as fans prepare for a slate of high-profile matchups that will determine which teams advance to the Elite Eight.

Rick Pitino has reached the Sweet 16 for the 14th time in his coaching career, extending a legacy spanning Providence, Kentucky, Louisville, and now St. John's.
St. John's victory over defending champion UConn ended a 41-year Sweet 16 drought dating back to 1985, marking a historic breakthrough for the program.
The win at Barclays Center on Sunday night is framed as a full-scale revival for St. John's, restoring national relevance and shifting the focus from past struggles to current potential.
Rick Pitino's coaching style is characterized by disciplined defense, quick turnarounds, and composure under pressure, traits that defined his deep runs at Kentucky and Louisville.
St. John's advancement provides a significant visibility boost for the Big East conference, aligning with the league's branding around coaching, toughness, and postseason credibility.
The 2026 tournament run is portrayed as a compelling narrative of adaptability and staying power, with the team's success attributed to Pitino's ability to restore belief in a proud program.
The post concludes by framing Sunday night's victory not just as a tournament step, but as a definitive arrival for both Pitino and St. John's in the national conversation.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core event (St. John's 72-68 win over UConn to reach the Sweet 16) and Pitino's 14th appearance, but it materially distorts the opponent from the evidence's 'Omaha' to 'UConn' and frames the victory as a historic 41-year drought end without evidence supporting that specific claim.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims St. John's defeated 'defending national champion UConn' and ended a drought since 1985, whereas evidence [2] and [6] explicitly state the opponent was 'Omaha' and provide no corroboration for a 1985 Sweet 16 drought.
t1_problematic_subclaims: St. John's defeated defending national champion UConn
The Red Storm had not made the Sweet 16 since 1985
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post inflates the narrative by calling the win a 'historic breakthrough' that ended a '41-year drought' and a 'full-scale revival', while evidence [2] and [6] describe it merely as a 'notable gap' and a 'significant step' in a 'broader resurgence' without confirming the 1985 date or 'historic' status.
t2_problematic_subclaims: ended a 41-year Sweet 16 drought dating back to 1985
marking a historic breakthrough for the program
text_analysis.t3_evidence: The post frames the game as a win over 'UConn' and implies a specific 'defending national champion' context, whereas evidence [2] and [6] place the game against 'Omaha', creating a wrong opponent context.
t3_problematic_subclaims: win over defending national champion UConn
text_analysis.c1_evidence: The post text and image descriptions claim the opponent was 'UConn' and the game was against a 'defending national champion', while image 2 (02_img_2.jpg) OCR and entities explicitly show the opponent as 'Omaha' and the text '6 ST. JOHN'S 83 | 57 11 OMAHA'.
c1_problematic_subclaims: St. John's win over UConn (Image 2 shows Omaha)
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is a high-quality narrative piece that correctly identifies Rick Pitino and St. John's advancement but fabricates the opponent (UConn vs. Omaha) and invents a specific historical drought date (1985) not supported by the evidence.

images.scene_description: A close-up shot of Rick Pitino on the sidelines with his mouth open, shouting or reacting intensely. He is wearing a blue suit and a red patterned tie. The background is blurred but shows spectators and a 'MARCH MADNESS' sign.
images.explanation: A clean, authentic photo of Rick Pitino reacting during a game, matching the post's description.

images.scene_description: A graphic poster for the 2025 NCAA Tournament showing a bracket. It highlights St. John's advancing to the Sweet 16 under Rick Pitino, showing a win over Omaha and an upcoming game against Tennessee. It features a photo of Rick Pitino and the St. John's logo.
images.ocr_text: 2025 NCAA TOURNAMENT | SWEET 16 CLINCHED! | ST. JOHN'S ADVANCES UNDER RICK PITINO | SECOND ROUND | 6 ST. JOHN'S 83 57 11 OMAHA | UP NEXT: SWEET 16 | 6 ST. JOHN'S VS 4 TENNESSEE | THURSDAY, MARCH 27 | 7:09 PM ET | tbs
images.v2_evidence: The image is a composite graphic poster with large headlines ('SWEET 16 CLINCHED!'), a bracket diagram, and a photo of Rick Pitino, which is a form of visual editing/overlay as defined by V2.
images.c_evidence: The image depicts a 2025 tournament scenario (St. John's vs. Omaha, next vs. Tennessee), while the post text claims a 2026 tournament win over UConn to reach the Sweet 16, creating a mismatch in year, opponent, and event.
images.explanation: The image is a graphic poster for a different year (2025) and a different matchup (vs. Omaha) than the one described in the post (2026 vs. UConn), indicating a significant contextual and semantic mismatch.

images.scene_description: The official logo of the St. John's University Red Storm basketball team, featuring the letters 'SJ' in red with a black outline.
images.explanation: A clean, official logo image that correctly represents the team discussed in the post.

images.scene_description: Rick Pitino on the sidelines during a game, wearing a dark suit and red tie, with his hand to his mouth. Two players in white jerseys with 'HARRELL 24' and 'ROZIER 0' are visible in the foreground.
images.explanation: A clean, authentic photo of Rick Pitino on the sidelines with his players, matching the post's description.

image_analysis.global_notes: Image 2 is a graphic poster for a different year (2025) and a different matchup (vs. Omaha) than the one described in the post (2026 vs. UConn), indicating a significant contextual and semantic mismatch.

judge.level3_rationale: The post is False because it fabricates the opponent and score of the game (claiming a win over UConn 72-68 instead of Omaha 83-57), a core factual error that is directly contradicted by the provided graphic image and news evidence, rendering the central claim entirely unfounded.
judge.reasoning_trace: The text claims a win over UConn 72-68, but evidence and Image 2 confirm a win over Omaha 83-57. This is a T1 Fabrication and C1/C2 mismatch. The dramatic framing is T2. The combination of T1 and multiple C labels makes the verdict False.
judge.core_claim: St. John's defeated defending national champion UConn 72-68 on Sunday night at Barclays Center to advance Rick Pitino's team to the Sweet 16 for the 14th time in the 2026 NCAA tournament.
key_subclaims: St. John's defeated defending national champion UConn 72-68.
The game took place on Sunday night at Barclays Center.
Rick Pitino is advancing to the Sweet 16 for the 14th time.
St. John's had not made the Sweet 16 since 1985.
The graphic shows St. John's 72-68 win over UConn and the next matchup against Tennessee.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: St. John's defeated defending national champion UConn 72-68.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence explicitly states the opponent was Omaha, not UConn, and the score was 83-57.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The game took place on Sunday night at Barclays Center.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the event but does not specify the venue or time of day.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Rick Pitino is advancing to the Sweet 16 for the 14th time.
St. John's had not made the Sweet 16 since 1985.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence mentions the program's resurgence but does not confirm the specific 1985 drought date.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The graphic shows St. John's 72-68 win over UConn and the next matchup against Tennessee.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 2 explicitly shows a win over Omaha (83-57) and the next matchup against Tennessee, contradicting the text's claim of a win over UConn.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post fabricates the opponent (UConn vs. Omaha) and score (72-68 vs. 83-57), which is a T1 Fabrication. The image (Image 2) explicitly contradicts the text by showing the correct opponent and score, leading to C1 Semantic Inconsistency and C2 Contextual Inconsistency. The text also uses dramatic language ('historic breakthrough', 'full-scale revival') that overstates the evidence, triggering T2. With T1, V2, and multiple C labels, the post is False.

image_findings.explanation: Image shows Pitino reacting, consistent with the post's description of the event, though the specific game details are wrong.
Image shows a win over Omaha (83-57), directly contradicting the text's claim of a win over UConn (72-68).
Image is the St. John's logo, which aligns with the team mentioned in the text.
Image shows Pitino on the sideline, consistent with the post's description of his actions.
